title: Measurements and Uncertainty
description: SI base and derived units, significant figures and orders of magnitude, systematic and random error, and how to calculate and combine absolute, fractional and percentage uncertainties — including reading a gradient's uncertainty from a graph.
objectives: Recall the seven SI base units and construct derived units (e.g. N, J, W, Pa, V, Ω) from them
Use standard form, unit prefixes, significant figures and order-of-magnitude estimates appropriately
Distinguish systematic from random error and describe practical ways to reduce each
Calculate the absolute, fractional and percentage uncertainty in a single measurement
Combine uncertainties for sums/differences, products/quotients and powers of measured quantities
Find the uncertainty in a gradient and an intercept from a best-fit line using the max/min gradient method

lessons.title: SI units, prefixes and significant figures
